calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Determing Title To Real Property Located At 654 28th, S.F. Ca
Off Calendar Oct-17-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances - Phil Foster for Tom Mogensen, Trustee, petitioner; John O'Grady for ArtSeed, beneficiary; and Josefa Vaughan, executive director of ArtSeed.
The Court asked Counsel to address the litigation in France. Mr. Foster stated that the French heirs filed a petition in France asserting they were the rightful heirs to the decedent's property in France based on a French Will. In addition, the French beneficiaries filed a petition here, case #287582, seeking to revoke probate of the decedent's will or an order stating the decedent did not intend for her U.S. Will to govern disposition of her property in France. The Court asked if the 1995 trust revoked the 1989 trust. Mr. Foster stated that the 1995 trust did not revoke the trust, but that later wills addressed revocation. The Court asked if in fact, the Will contest was germane to this petition, as the property might pass differently if probate of the will was revoked. Mr. Foster stated that the Petition to Revoke the Will was entirely separate as the Trustor intended that the real property at issue in this petition was listed as an asset of both trusts and listed in several amendments, clearly indicating the decedent created a trust over the property and that there were no circumstances or evidence showing the decedent actually did or intended to transfer the property out of the trust. He stated he was just seeking an order clarifying the identification of the grantor in the deed that transferred the property to herself as Trustee of the 1995 trust. The current deed is signed by the decedent as an individual and he needs an order showing that the grantor was the decedent acting as trustee of the 1989 trust, not as an individual. He stated the French beneficiaries received notice of the petition and did not file objections. The Court took the petition under submission.
Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Sandy Hilton.
